name: LLaMA 2
brainrotOneliner: 70B parameters, actually open, grouped-query attention. meta handed the community a main character and said go crazy.

brainrotStats.attentionHeads: 64 heads (GQA means fewer KV heads tho)

keyInnovations: Grouped-Query Attention (GQA) — reduces KV cache size significantly without losing quality
Ghost Attention for multi-turn instruction following consistency
Released with chat-tuned variants and safety-fine-tuned versions
First widely-available open-weight model competitive with GPT-3.5 class models
brainrotSummary: LLaMA 2 is the model that made open-source actually competitive. grouped-query attention was a real architectural improvement, not just a scaling trick. the 70B variant went toe-to-toe with GPT-3.5 class models. meta dropped this and the entire fine-tuning ecosystem exploded overnight. open-source protagonist arc fully unlocked.

comparisons.gpt2: LLaMA 2 took everything GPT-2 established and made it open, efficient, and actually useful. evolution arc.
comparisons.gpt3: LLaMA 2 is what happens when Meta decides everyone should have access to a GPT-3 class model. democratization arc.
comparisons.deepseek-v3: LLaMA 2 opened the era. DeepSeek V3 is what happens when you take the open-source recipe and scale up the ambition.
comparisons.gpt4: LLaMA 2 is the open-source challenger. GPT-4 is still ahead but the gap closed faster than anyone expected.
comparisons.claude3: two different philosophies. Meta says open everything. Anthropic says build it right first.
comparisons.llama3-instruct: LLaMA 2 is the prototype. LLaMA 3 Instruct is the refined version from the same team, one year wiser.
comparisons.mixtral-8x7b: LLaMA 2 is dense. Mixtral took the community momentum and added MoE routing. different efficiency approaches.
comparisons.grok-1: LLaMA 2 is Meta's open bet. Grok-1 is xAI's chaotic energy bet. both trying to compete with OpenAI differently.
